item.title: Microsoft Copilot Is Confronting Its Identity Crisis
item.description: The AI assistant needs a more consistent personality

item.title: Light at End of the Tunnel for China’s Property Slump
item.description: Bloomberg Economics reckons China’s property slump could bottom out in 2027

item.title: Nasdaq Seeks to Build Crypto Into Wall Street’s Market Plumbing
item.description: The operators of Wall Street’s oldest exchanges are racing to wire crypto infrastructure into the traditional financial system, betting that the two markets are headed toward a round-the-clock ecosystem.

item.title: Pfizer Says Long-Awaited Lyme Vaccine Was Effective in Study
item.description: Pfizer Inc. said its experimental Lyme disease vaccine was 73% effective against the tick-borne illness, though fewer-than-expected cases in a study made it hard to determine how well it works on a large scale.

item.title: The Mood in Markets Is Sell First, Ask Questions Later
item.description: Yields are climbing everywhere as traders price in higher inflation.

item.title: US Two-Year Bond Yield Climbs to 4% for First Time Since June
item.description: The two-year US Treasury yield surged to 4% for the first time since June amid a global selloff in government debt as the war in the Middle East intensifies.

item.title: Sony Said Near $1 Billion Home Entertainment Deal With TCL
item.description: Sony Group Corp. is nearing a binding agreement to sell a majority stake in its home entertainment business to Chinese rival TCL Electronics Holdings Ltd. in a deal that may be valued at about $1 billion, according to people familiar with the matter.

item.title: French Banks Have EU’s Top Middle East Exposure, EBA Says
item.description: French banks had about €60.8 billion ($69.9 billion) in direct exposures to counterparties located in the Middle East at the end of last year, according to the European Union’s banking regulator.

item.title: Indra Chairman Secures Rare Union Support in Clash With Sanchez
item.description: Spain’s largest union backed Indra Sistemas SA’s Chairman Ángel Escribano, warning that efforts to oust him risk destabilizing the state-backed defense company at a critical moment for Europe’s military buildup.

item.title: HPE Gets Day in Court as State AGs Challenge $14 Billion Deal
item.description: Hewlett Packard Enterprise Inc. faces a crucial hurdle Monday to reaping the benefits of its $14 billion takeover of wireless networking rival Juniper Networks Inc., as a federal judge considers whether the Trump administration improperly approved an antitrust settlement that a group of states claim was tainted.

item.title: Modi Seeks to Calm India as Iran War Causes Acute Gas Shortage
item.description: India’s Prime Minister Narendra Modi said the country has sufficient energy supplies to meet domestic demand, even as he warned the Iran conflict creates unprecedented challenges for the world’s fastest growing large economy.

item.title: We’re All Just ‘Monitoring the Situation’
item.description: Vibe-coded dashboards elevate the online news scroll to a different level.

item.title: Sinopec to Ensure China’s Fuel Supply and Cut Chemicals Losses
item.description: China’s biggest oil refiner said it will prioritize ensuring domestic fuel supplies as it braces for a prolonged conflict in the Middle East.

item.title: Fink Says AI Threatens to Leave Masses Behind Unless They Invest
item.description: BlackRock Inc. Chief Executive Officer Larry Fink warned that the artificial intelligence boom threatens to make wealthy companies and investors even richer while exacerbating inequality, unless more individuals can share in the market gains.

item.title: Lead Edge Capital Raises $3.5 Billion for Software-Focused Fund
item.description: Lead Edge Capital, a growth equity firm that has backed ByteDance Ltd., Spotify Technology SA and startup Grafana Labs, has raised $3.5 billion for its seventh fund to focus on software investments even as other investors dump stocks in the sector.

item.title: Investors Game Out Iran Risk as Clock Ticks on Trump’s Deadline
item.description: Investors are weighing sharply diverging outcomes from Donald Trump’s demand for Iran to reopen the Strait of Hormuz, underscoring how the latest standoff is looming as a turning point.

item.title: UAE Restarts Main Gas Supply Plant While Idling Most LNG Output
item.description: The United Arab Emirates resumed operations at its largest natural gas processing plant, after an attack last week had forced a halt to the facility that’s vital for supplying much of the country’s requirements, according to a person familiar with the situation.

item.title: Euro-Zone Wage Growth to Quicken in Second Half of Next Year
item.description: Euro-area pay growth is set to accelerate in the second half, adding to the challenges the European Central Bank faces as war rages in the Middle East.

item.title: Hedge Funds Feed Dollar Gains as US, Iran Exchange Threats
item.description: The dollar advanced and traders bet on further gains for the reserve currency as the war in the Middle East entered its fourth week and the US exchanged threats with Iran.

item.title: Strategists Unfazed by War Expect European Stocks to Bounce
item.description: Market strategists are sanguine about mounting inflation fears caused by the Iran war and expect European stocks to return to record highs, according to a Bloomberg survey.

item.title: Laopu Gold Sales, Profit Beat as Gold Rally Fuels Demand
item.description: Laopu Gold Co.’s full-year revenue and profit surged as its jewelry pieces and gold ornaments gained huge popularity from the country’s middle class shoppers, aided by soaring gold prices.

item.title: Dangote Ships Fuel to Five Nations After Reaching Full Capacity
item.description: Nigeria’s Dangote Petroleum Refinery and Petrochemicals has begun exporting fuel across Africa after reaching full production, shipping about a dozen cargoes to markets as far as Tanzania.

item.title: China Copper Inventories Plunge as Falling Prices Aid Demand
item.description: Chinese copper inventories had their biggest weekly drop this year, according to Mysteel Global, as a rapid slump in prices of the metal due to the Iran war buoyed demand.

item.title: European Stocks Sink Into Correction on Trump’s Iran Escalation
item.description: European stocks slumped, with the Stoxx Europe 600 Index on course for a correction from its February record high, as the conflict in the Middle East escalated.

item.title: Bonds Lose $2.5 Trillion in Iran War Wipeout That Mirrors 2022
item.description: The specter of stagflation caused by the Iran war has wiped out more than $2.5 trillion from the value of global bonds in March, on track for the biggest monthly loss in more than three years.

item.title: Traders Fully Price Four Quarter-Point Hikes From BOE in 2026
item.description: Traders are fully pricing a full percentage point of Bank of England monetary tightening in 2026 for the first time, as the Iran conflict continues to fuel inflationary pressures.

item.title: Danone to Buy UK Protein Drinks Maker Huel in Nutrition Push
item.description: Danone SA will acquire UK-based fortified drinks maker Huel, as part of the French food company’s push into functional nutrition.

item.title: What the Iran War Means for Dubai’s Luxury Boom
item.description: The future of Gulf capital.

item.title: Balderton-Backed Startup Dash0 Bags $110 Million to Expand in US
item.description: Dash0 has raised $110 million at a $1 billion valuation in a Balderton Capital-led round to expand its monitoring platform, used to detect problems in software systems, across the US.

item.title: UBS Could Slow Investments on Iran War Uncertainty, Ermotti Says
item.description: UBS Group AG Chief Executive Officer Sergio Ermotti said the Iran war could force him to pare back spending, although it won’t fundamentally alter the bank’s overall strategy.
